shellCommands: for f in data/*.json; do printf '%s ' "$f"; jq 'if type=="array" then length else keys end' "$f"; done

cat > package.json <<'EOF' {"scripts":{"dev":"vite --host 0.0.0.0","build":"vite build && rm -rf dist/data && cp -R data dist/data","preview":"vite preview --host 0.0.0.0"},"dependencies":{"@vitejs/plugin-basic-ssl":"latest","vite":"latest","typescript":"latest","three":"latest"},"devDependencies":{}} EOF cat > index.html <<'EOF' Near-Earth Objects
EOF mkdir -p src
which chromium || which chromium-browser || which google-chrome || which "Google Chrome" || ls /Applications | rg -i 'chrome|chromium'
finalMessage:
Implemented the PLAN.md as a complete static Three.js app.

What’s included:
- 3D solar system with Sun, planets, full 42,075 asteroid catalog, and closed-orbit comet overlay.
- Orbital propagation from runtime-fetched `data/*.json`.
- Interactive time controls, date scrub, play/pause, speed.
- Search and filters for PHA, Sentry, close approaches, large objects, low MOID.
- Selected-object detail panel with orbit, size, hazard, Sentry risk, close approaches, highlighted orbit, follow mode.
- Relative asset/data paths and `dist/data/` copied during build.
- Short implementation notes in [README.md](/Users/mikevalstar/projects/nbench/2026-06-19-20-44-13-codex-gpt-5-5-medium/README.md).

Verification:
- `pnpm build` succeeds and emits `dist/`.
- Served build was checked in Chrome at desktop and mobile sizes.
- Browser check loaded the full catalog with no console errors and confirmed nonblank canvas rendering.

Only note: Vite reports the JS chunk is over 500 kB because Three.js is bundled. The build still succeeds.
